text:
INDICES — VIX 25.3, down 1.86% on the day (VIXY proxy), sitting mid-range vs. 52-week 13.5–52.3. Today's return ranking: IWM +1.22%, QQQ +0.66%, DIA +0.64%, SPY +0.56%. Small caps outperformed large caps. TLT +0.96% and GLD +3.01% rallied in tandem with equities, an unusual risk-on/safe-haven bid likely driven by dollar weakness concerns despite UUP +0.18%.

SECTORS — XLB +1.98%, XLV +1.00%, XLY +0.96%, XLI +0.67%, XLP +0.49%, XLK +0.45%, XLU +0.35%, XLC +0.28%, XLF +0.12%, XLRE -0.05%, XLE -0.44%. Top industry ETFs: REMX +5.35% and WGMI +3.68%. Bottom: BOAT -1.26% and MSOS -1.32%. XLB's +1.98% lead reflects the metals bid with silver and gold surging; XLE's -0.44% decline mirrors WTI crude falling -1.00% despite Brent elevated at $103.79.

THE 7s — Semi +3.64%, New Metals +2.12%, Old Metals +2.03%, Defense +1.12%, Health +1.09%, Buildout +1.03%, Housing +0.94%, Old Staples +0.78%, Freight +0.78%, Bank +0.70%, Industrial +0.65%, Cloud +0.58%, New Staples +0.45%, Power +0.40%, Medtech +0.36%, Energy +0.08%, Insurance -0.15%, Toll Road -0.22%, Fab -0.92%, Software -1.17%. Strongest individual in Semi basket led the tape; Software was the session's weakest basket, down -1.17% with a brutal -25.68% YTD hole.

SIGNALS — Fear & Greed 51.5, Neutral. Vol spikes: IJK 16.9x, IJS 12.8x, XHE 4.4x, ARGT 3.0x, ROBO 2.5x. 20-day trend leader: Energy basket +13.13%; laggard: Old Metals -19.17%. Best sector 20d: XLE +10.39%; worst: XLP -8.43%. No confirmed catalyst available for March 26; XLB's session leadership aligns with the metals complex surge, with silver +3.59% and gold +3.01% driving materials outperformance.
